mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which raises corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0% reflects restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal simultaneous weakness in consumer spending and residential investment, which together predict negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of joint-decline instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a negative 3-month change in industrial production (INDPRO) signals capacity underutilization and margin compression, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO 3-month change is negative during late-cycle expansions but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and year-over-year consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) generate stagflationary pressure that erodes corporate earnings and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 2 months and CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%
failure_condition: The joint trigger fires but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in advanced retail sales (RSAFS) captures consumer spending momentum that translates directly into revenue growth for S&P 500 constituents, predicting positive forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of weeks over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals accelerating labor market deterioration that triggers recession expectations and risk-off equity positioning over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: economic expansion with tightening labor constraints
failure_condition: UNRATE 3-month change exceeds 0.3pp but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in housing starts (HOUST) signal deteriorating residential investment and forward-looking weakness in construction and durable goods demand, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines for 2 consecutive months but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.
